general.g_text_audio:
여자: 민수 씨, 이번 주말에 ‘사랑나누기’ 모임이 있는데 같이 가실래요? 
남자: ‘사랑나누기’요? 그게 뭐예요? 
여자: 혼자 사시는 할머니들을 도와 드리는 모임이에요. 
남자: 아, 그래요? 근데 무슨 일을 도와 드려요? 
여자: 청소를 하거나 음식을 만들어 드려요. 이번 주말엔 김치를 담가서 드릴 거예요. 
남자: 좋은 일을 하시네요. 저도 이번 모임에 가 보고 싶어요. 김치를 담가 본 적은 없지만 열심히 해 볼게요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Anh Minsoo, cuối tuần này có một cuộc họp mặt tên là "Chia sẻ tình yêu thương", anh có muốn đi cùng không? 
Nam: "Chia sẻ tình yêu thương" ư? Đó là gì vậy nhỉ? 
Nữ: Đó là cuộc họp mặt để giúp đỡ những bà cụ neo đơn sống một mình. 
Nam: Ồ, vậy sao? Nhưng chúng ta có thể giúp gì cho các cụ? 
Nữ: Chúng ta giúp các cụ dọn dẹp hoặc làm đồ ăn. Cuối tuần này bọn tôi định sẽ làm kimchi. 
Nam: Quả là một việc làm tốt đó. Tôi cùng muốn thử đi tới cuộc gặp này. Tôi chưa từng làm kim chi nhưng sẽ cố gắng.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: Minsoo, there's a gathering called "Sharing the Love" this weekend, do you want to come with us?
Man: "Share the love"? What is that?
Woman: It's a gathering to help elderly women who live alone.
Man: Oh, really? But what can we do to help?
Woman: We clean up or make food. We're going to make kimchi this weekend.
Man: You guys are doing good things. I want to go to this gathering, too. I've never made kimchi, but I'll try my best.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Minsoo, il y a un rassemblement appelé "Sharing the Love" ce week-end, tu veux venir avec nous ?
Homme : "Partager l'amour" ? Qu'est-ce que c'est?
Femme : C'est un rassemblement pour aider les femmes âgées qui vivent seules.
Homme : Oh, vraiment ? Mais que pouvons-nous faire pour aider ?
Femme : Nous nettoyons ou préparons de la nourriture. Nous allons faire du kimchi ce week-end.
Homme : Vous faites de bonnes choses. Je veux aussi aller à ce rassemblement. Je n'ai jamais fait de kimchi, mais je ferai de mon mieux.

#######

Woman: Minsoo, there's a gathering called "Sharing the Love" this weekend, do you want to come with us?
Man: "Share the love"? What is that?
Woman: It's a gathering to help elderly women who live alone.
Man: Oh, really? But what can we do to help?
Woman: We clean up or make food. We're going to make kimchi this weekend.
Man: You guys are doing good things. I want to go to this gathering, too. I've never made kimchi, but I'll try my best.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Mujer: Minsoo, hay una reunión llamada "Sharing the Love" este fin de semana, ¿quieres venir con nosotros?
Hombre: ¿"Compartir el amor"? ¿Qué es eso?
Mujer: Es un encuentro para ayudar a mujeres mayores que viven solas.
Hombre: Ah, ¿en serio? Pero, ¿qué podemos hacer para ayudar?
Mujer: Limpiamos o hacemos la comida. Vamos a hacer kimchi este fin de semana.
Hombre: Ustedes están haciendo cosas buenas. Yo también quiero ir a esta reunión. Nunca he hecho kimchi, pero haré lo mejor que pueda.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.id:
Wanita: Minsoo, ada pertemuan yang disebut "Berbagi Cinta" akhir pekan ini, apakah kamu mau ikut dengan kami?
Pria: "Bagikan cinta"? Apa itu?
Wanita: Ini adalah pertemuan untuk membantu wanita lanjut usia yang hidup sendiri.
Pria: Oh, benarkah? Tapi apa yang bisa kita lakukan untuk membantu?
Wanita: Kami membersihkan atau membuat makanan. Kami akan membuat kimchi akhir pekan ini.
Pria: Kalian melakukan hal-hal baik. Saya ingin pergi ke pertemuan ini juga. Saya belum pernah membuat kimchi, tapi saya akan mencoba yang terbaik.

g_text_audio_translate.ja:
女性: ミンス、今週末は「愛を分かち合う」という集まりがあるんだけど、一緒に来ない？
男：「愛を分かち合う」？それは何ですか？
女：一人暮らしの高齢女性を助けるための集まりです。
男: あ、そうですか。しかし、私たちは何を助けることができるでしょうか?
女: 片付けたり、料理を作ったりします。今週末はキムチを作ろう。
男：君たちはいいことをしているね。私もこの集まりに行きたいです。キムチ作ったことないけど頑張ります。

#######

g_text_audio_translate.de:
Frau: Minsoo, dieses Wochenende gibt es ein Treffen namens „Sharing the Love“, willst du mit uns kommen?
Mann: "Teile die Liebe"? Was ist das?
Frau: Es ist eine Versammlung, um älteren Frauen zu helfen, die allein leben.
Mann: Ach, wirklich? Aber was können wir tun, um zu helfen?
Frau: Wir räumen auf oder machen Essen. Wir werden dieses Wochenende Kimchi machen.
Mann: Ihr macht gute Sachen. Ich möchte auch zu dieser Versammlung gehen. Ich habe noch nie Kimchi gemacht, aber ich werde mein Bestes geben.

g_text_audio_translate.zh-CN:
女：珉秀，这个周末有个聚会叫“分享爱”，你要不要跟我们一起去？
男：“分享爱”？那是什么？
女人：这是一个帮助独居老年妇女的聚会。
男：哦，真的吗？但是我们能做些什么来提供帮助呢？
女人：我们打扫卫生或做食物。这个周末我们要做泡菜。
男：你们做得很好。我也想去参加这个聚会。我从来没有做过泡菜，但我会尽力而为。

g_text_audio_translate.it:
Donna: Minsoo, c'è un raduno chiamato "Sharing the Love" questo fine settimana, vuoi venire con noi?
Uomo: "Condividi l'amore"? Cos'è quello?
Donna: È un raduno per aiutare le donne anziane che vivono da sole.
Uomo: Ah, davvero? Ma cosa possiamo fare per aiutare?
Donna: Puliamo o prepariamo il cibo. Faremo il kimchi questo fine settimana.
Uomo: Voi ragazzi state facendo cose buone. Anch'io voglio andare a questo raduno. Non ho mai fatto il kimchi, ma farò del mio meglio.

g_text_audio_translate.ru:
Женщина: Минсу, на этих выходных состоится собрание под названием «Разделяем любовь», хочешь пойти с нами?
Мужчина: «Поделись любовью»? Что это такое?
Женщина: Это собрание, чтобы помочь одиноким пожилым женщинам.
Мужчина: О, правда? Но чем мы можем помочь?
Женщина: Мы убираемся или готовим еду. На этих выходных мы собираемся приготовить кимчи.
Мужчина: Ребята, вы делаете хорошие дела. Я тоже хочу пойти на это собрание. Я никогда не готовила кимчи, но постараюсь изо всех сил.

g_text_audio_translate.th:
ผู้หญิง: มินซู สุดสัปดาห์นี้มีงานที่เรียกว่า "Sharing the Love" ไปด้วยกันไหม?
ผู้ชาย: "แบ่งปันความรัก"? นั่นคืออะไร?
ผู้หญิง: เป็นการรวมตัวกันเพื่อช่วยเหลือผู้หญิงสูงอายุที่อยู่คนเดียว
ผู้ชาย: โอ้จริงเหรอ? แต่เราสามารถช่วยอะไรได้บ้าง?
ผู้หญิง: เราทำความสะอาดหรือทำอาหาร เราจะทำกิมจิสุดสัปดาห์นี้
ผู้ชาย: พวกคุณทำสิ่งดีๆ อยากไปงานรวมพลครั้งนี้ด้วย ฉันไม่เคยทำกิมจิ แต่ฉันจะพยายามทำให้ดีที่สุด

g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Mulher: Minsoo, há uma reunião chamada "Compartilhando o Amor" neste fim de semana, você quer vir conosco?
Homem: "Compartilhe o amor"? O que é aquilo?
Mulher: É um encontro para ajudar mulheres idosas que moram sozinhas.
Homem: Ah, é mesmo? Mas o que podemos fazer para ajudar?
Mulher: Nós limpamos ou fazemos comida. Vamos fazer kimchi neste fim de semana.
Homem: Vocês estão fazendo coisas boas. Eu quero ir a esta reunião também. Nunca fiz kimchi, mas vou tentar o meu melhor.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
महिला: मिन्सू, इस सप्ताह के अंत में "शेयरिंग द लव" नामक एक सभा है, क्या आप हमारे साथ आना चाहते हैं?
आदमी: "प्यार बांटो"? वह क्या है?
महिला: यह उन बुजुर्ग महिलाओं की मदद करने के लिए एक सभा है जो अकेली रहती हैं।
आदमी: ओह, सच में? लेकिन हम मदद के लिए क्या कर सकते हैं?
महिला: हम सफाई करते हैं या खाना बनाते हैं। हम इस सप्ताहांत किमची बनाने जा रहे हैं।
आदमी: तुम लोग अच्छा काम कर रहे हो. मैं भी इस सभा में जाना चाहता हूं. मैंने कभी किमची नहीं बनाई है, लेकिन मैं अपना सर्वश्रेष्ठ प्रयास करूंगा।

g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Kadın: Minsoo, bu hafta sonu "Sevgiyi Paylaşmak" adında bir toplantı var, bizimle gelmek ister misin?
Adam: "Sevgiyi paylaşmak" mı? Bu nedir?
Kadın: Yalnız yaşayan yaşlı kadınlara yardım etmek için yapılan bir toplantı.
Adam: Ah, gerçekten mi? Peki yardım etmek için ne yapabiliriz?
Kadın: Temizlik yapıyoruz ya da yemek yapıyoruz. Bu hafta sonu kimchi yapacağız.
Adam: İyi şeyler yapıyorsunuz. Ben de bu toplantıya gitmek istiyorum. Hiç kimchi yapmadım ama elimden geleni yapacağım.

explain.vi:
Tại sao hai người này lại làm kim chi? 
1. Vì muốn giúp đỡ những bà cụ
2. Vì muốn ăn cùng những bà cụ
3. Vì muốn thử làm kim chi một lần
4. Vì làm kimchi một mình thì vất vả
--------------------
Bạn nữ tham gia nhóm giúp đỡ những bà cụ sống một mình. Tuần này họ sẽ làm kimchi giúp những bà cụ đó.
explain.en:
Why are these two making kimchi?
1. Because they want to help grandmothers
2. Because they want to eat kimchi with grandmothers
3. Because they want to try making kimchi once
4. Because it's hard to make kimchi yourself
--------------------
The woman joins a group to help elderly women who live alone. This weekend they will make kimchi for those elderly women.
explain.fr:
Pourquoi ces deux-là font-ils du kimchi ?
1. Parce qu'ils veulent aider les grands-mères
2. Parce qu'ils veulent manger du kimchi avec leurs grands-mères
3. Parce qu'ils veulent essayer de faire du kimchi une fois
4. Parce qu'il est difficile de faire soi-même du kimchi
--------------------
La femme rejoint un groupe pour aider les femmes âgées qui vivent seules. Ce week-end, ils feront du kimchi pour ces femmes âgées.

#######

<g>저도 이번 모임에 가 보고 싶어요. 김치를 담가 본 적은 없지만 열심히 해 볼게요. </g> The man said that he wanted to go to the gathering and would try his best to make kimchi even though he had never made it.

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 아이 통장을 하나 만들려고 하는데요. 
남자: 아, 어린이 통장요? 요즘 입학 때라 많이들 만드시네요. 
여자: 네. 우리 아이도 자기 통장을 갖고 싶어해서 선물하려고요. 
남자: 좋은 생각이세요. 자기 통장이 생기면 돈을 모으는 즐거움을 느낄 수 있을 거예요. 참, 아이 도장은 가져오셨죠? 
여자: 도장이 필요해요? 안 가져왔는데... 그냥 내일 다시 와야겠네요. 
남자: 그럼 내일 아이도 데리고 오세요. 은행에 와서 직접 통장을 만들면 더 좋아할 거예요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Tôi muốn làm sổ ngân hàng cho con. 
Nam: Ồ, sổ ngân hàng cho trẻ con ư? Dạo này là thời gian nhập học nên nhiều người làm nhỉ. 
Nữ: Vâng. Con tôi cũng muốn có sổ ngân hàng của riêng mình nên tôi định tặng cho nó. 
Nam: Ý tưởng hay đó. Nếu có sổ ngân hàng của riêng mình chúng có thể cảm nhận được niềm vui khi tiết kiệm được tiền. Để tôi xem nào, cô có mang theo con dấu riêng của con không? 
Nữ: Cần con dấu sao? Tôi không mang theo... Chắc là tôi phải quay lại vào ngày mai rồi. 
Nam: Vậy ngày mai cô hãy đưa cả cháu đến nữa. Nếu được trực tiếp tới mở sổ thì đứa bé sẽ càng thích nó hơn.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: I want to make a bankbook for my child.
Man: Oh, a children's bankbook? A lot of people make it these days because it's the admission season.
Woman: Yes. My kid also wants his own bankbook so I plan to give it to him as a present.
Man: That's a good idea. If they have their own bank book, they can feel the joy of saving money. By the way, did you bring your child's seal?
Woman: We need a seal? I didn't bring it... I guess I'll have to come back tomorrow.
Man: Then, bring your child with you tomorrow. He will like it more if he comes to the bank and makes his own bankbook.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Je veux faire un livret pour mon enfant.
Homme : Oh, un livret pour enfant ? Beaucoup de gens le font ces jours-ci parce que c'est la saison des admissions.
Femme : Oui. Mon enfant veut aussi son propre livret bancaire, alors je prévois de le lui offrir en cadeau.
Homme : C'est une bonne idée. S'ils ont leur propre livret bancaire, ils peuvent ressentir la joie d'économiser de l'argent. Au fait, avez-vous apporté le sceau de votre enfant ?
Femme : Nous avons besoin d'un sceau ? Je ne l'ai pas apporté... Je suppose que je devrai revenir demain.
Homme : Alors, amenez votre enfant avec vous demain. Il aimera davantage s'il vient à la banque et fait son propre livret.

ผู้หญิง: ฉันอยากทำสมุดบัญชีธนาคารให้ลูก

ผู้หญิง: เราต้องการตราประทับเหรอ? ฉันไม่ได้เอามา... สงสัยต้องกลับพรุ่งนี้

महिला: मैं अपने बच्चे के लिए एक बैंकबुक बनाना चाहती हूं।
आदमी: ओह, बच्चों की बैंकबुक? इन दिनों बहुत सारे लोग इसे बनाते हैं क्योंकि यह प्रवेश का मौसम है।
महिला: हाँ. मेरा बच्चा भी अपनी बैंकबुक चाहता है इसलिए मैं उसे उपहार के रूप में देने की योजना बना रहा हूं।
आदमी: यह एक अच्छा विचार है. यदि उनके पास अपनी बैंक बुक है, तो वे पैसे बचाने की खुशी महसूस कर सकते हैं। वैसे, क्या आप अपने बच्चे की मुहर लाये?
महिला: हमें मुहर चाहिए? मैं इसे नहीं लाया... मुझे लगता है मुझे कल वापस आना होगा।
आदमी: तो, कल अपने बच्चे को अपने साथ ले आओ। अगर वह बैंक में आकर अपनी बैंकबुक बनायेगा तो उसे अधिक अच्छा लगेगा।

content.q_text: 여자는 왜 아이에게 통장을 만들어 줍니까?

explain.vi:
Tại sao người phụ nữ muốn làm sổ ngân hàng cho con? 
1. Vì đứa trẻ muốn tiết kiệm tiền
2. Vì đứa trẻ có rất nhiều tiền
3. Vì đã đến lúc đứa trẻ phải đổi sổ ngân hàng 
4. Vì đứa trẻ muốn có sổ ngân hàng của riêng mình 
--------------------
통장: Sổ tài khoản, sổ ngân hàng
갖다: Có, cầm, sở hữu
explain.en:
Why does the woman want to make a bankbook for her child?
1. Because the child wants to save money
2. Because the child has a lot of money
3. Because it's time for the child to change his bankbook
4. Because the child wants to have his own bankbook
--------------------
<g>통장:</g> Bankbook
<g>갖다:</g> To have, to hold
explain.fr:
Pourquoi la femme veut-elle faire un livret pour son enfant ?
1. Parce que l'enfant veut économiser de l'argent
2. Parce que l'enfant a beaucoup d'argent
3. Parce que c'est le moment pour l'enfant de changer de livret
4. Parce que l'enfant veut avoir son propre livret bancaire
--------------------
<g>통장:</g>Livret de banque
<g>갖다:</g>Avoir, tenir

general.g_text_audio:
남자: 안녕하세요? 여기 행복마트인데요. 지난주에 주문한 그릇 때문에 전화 드렸습니다. 
여자: 네, 연락하려고 했는데 주문한 그릇이 왜 이렇게 안 와요? 
남자: 원하시는 색깔이 가게에 없어서 공장에 주문을 했는데요. 생각보다 늦어져서요. 죄송합니다. 
여자: 그래요? 그럼 언제쯤 받을 수 있어요? 
남자: 내일 보낼 거니까 모레쯤은 도착할 겁니다. 
여자: 네, 알겠습니다.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nam: Xin chào, đây là siêu thị Hạnh Phúc. Tôi gọi điện vì chiếc bát mà chị đã đặt tuần trước. 
Nữ: Vâng, tôi đã định liên lạc với anh đây, sao bát tôi đặt vẫn chưa đến vậy? 
Người đàn ông: Do màu chị muốn thì cửa hàng hiện đang không còn nên tôi đã đặt xưởng làm rồi ạ. Hàng đến muộn hơn so với dự định của chúng tôi. Rất xin lỗi quý khách. 
Người phụ nữ: Vậy sao? Vậy khi nào tôi có thể nhận được hàng? 
Người đàn ông: Ngày mai tôi sẽ gửi nên khoảng ngày kia hàng sẽ đến. 
Người phụ nữ: Vâng, tôi biết rồi.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Man: Hello, this is Happy mart. I'm calling about the bowl you ordered last week.
Woman: Oh, I was going to contact you, why the bowl I ordered didn't come yet?
Man: The color you wanted was not available in the store, so we had to order it from the factory. The bowl arrived later than we expected. We're sorry.
Woman: Is that so? So when can I get it?
Man: We'll send it tomorrow, so it'll arrive the day after tomorrow.
Woman: Oh, I see.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Homme : Bonjour, c'est Happy Mart. J'appelle pour le bol que vous avez commandé la semaine dernière.
Femme : Oh, j'allais vous contacter, pourquoi le bol que j'ai commandé n'est pas encore arrivé ?
Homme : La couleur que vous vouliez n'était pas disponible en magasin, nous avons donc dû la commander à l'usine. Le bol est arrivé plus tard que prévu. Nous sommes désolés.
Femme : C'est ça ? Alors, quand puis-je l'obtenir ?
Homme : Nous l'enverrons demain, donc il arrivera après-demain.
Femme : Oh, je vois.

#######

Man: Hello, this is Happy mart. I'm calling about the bowl you ordered last week.
Woman: Oh, I was going to contact you, why the bowl I ordered didn't come yet?
Man: The color you wanted was not available in the store, so we had to order it from the factory. The bowl arrived later than we expected. We're sorry.
Woman: Is that so? So when can I get it?
Man: We'll send it tomorrow, so it'll arrive the day after tomorrow.
Woman: Oh, I see.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Hombre: Hola, soy Happy Mart. Te llamo por el bol que pediste la semana pasada.
Mujer: Ay, te iba a contactar, ¿por qué no llegó el bol que pedí?
Hombre: El color que querías no estaba disponible en la tienda, así que tuvimos que pedirlo a la fábrica. El cuenco llegó más tarde de lo que esperábamos. Lo lamentamos.
Mujer: ¿Es así? Entonces, ¿cuándo puedo conseguirlo?
Hombre: Lo enviaremos mañana, así que llegará pasado mañana.
Mujer: Oh, ya veo.

#######

Homem: Olá, aqui é Happy Mart. Estou ligando por causa da tigela que você pediu na semana passada.

Homem: A cor que você queria não estava disponível na loja, então tivemos que encomendá-la na fábrica. A tigela chegou mais tarde do que esperávamos. Nós lamentamos.
Mulher: É mesmo? Então, quando posso obtê-lo?
Homem: Enviaremos amanhã, então chegará depois de amanhã.
Mulher: Ah, entendo.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
आदमी: हेलो, यह हैप्पी मार्ट है। मैं उस कटोरे के बारे में बात कर रहा हूँ जिसका आपने पिछले सप्ताह ऑर्डर किया था।
महिला: ओह, मैं आपसे संपर्क करने जा रही थी, मैंने जो कटोरा ऑर्डर किया था वह अभी तक क्यों नहीं आया?
आदमी: जो रंग आप चाहते थे वह स्टोर में उपलब्ध नहीं था, इसलिए हमें इसे फ़ैक्टरी से मंगवाना पड़ा। कटोरा हमारी अपेक्षा से देर से आया। हम क्षमा चाहते हैं।
महिला: क्या ऐसा है? तो मैं इसे कब प्राप्त कर सकता हूँ?
आदमी: हम इसे कल भेजेंगे, तो यह परसों आ जाएगा।
महिला: ओह, मैं समझ गयी.

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 선생님, 안녕하세요? 요즘 저희 아이가 책을 잘 안 읽어요. 그래서 걱정이 돼서 왔어요. 
남자: 네. 여기 앉으세요. (잠시 쉬고) 음... 아이가 책 읽는 걸 싫어하면 만화책부터 보여 주는 건 어떨까요? 
여자: 만화책요? 그러면 아이가 만화책만 좋아하지 않을까요? 
남자: 아니에요. 만화책이 책을 읽는 데 도움이 돼요. 만화책에서 본 내용이 재미있으면 다른 책도 찾아서 읽게 되니까요. 
여자: 아, 그러면 책 읽는 습관을 기를 수 있어서 좋을 것 같네요. 
남자: 네. 또 어려운 내용을 쉽게 이해할 수 있어서 공부에 도움도 돼요. 그래서 요즘 아이들은 만화책을 많이 읽어요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Xin chào thầy giáo. Dạo này con tôi không hay đọc sách. Tôi lo quá nên đến đây. 
Nam: Vâng. Chị ngồi đây đi. (Nghỉ một chút) Ừm... Nếu cháu nó không thích đọc sách thì chị cho cháu đọc thử truyện tranh xem sao? 
Nữ: Truyện tranh? Không phải nếu vậy thì trẻ con sẽ chỉ thích truyện tranh thôi sao? 
Nam: Không phải vậy đâu. Truyện tranh sẽ giúp ích cho việc đọc sách. Nếu nội dung trong truyện tranh thú vị thì bọn trẻ sẽ tìm những cuốn sách khác để đọc. 
Nữ: À, nếu vậy thì có thể xây dựng thói quen đọc sách nên rất tốt. 
Nam: Vâng. Ngoài ra, bọn trẻ có thể dễ dàng hiểu được nội dung khó nên cũng giúp ích cho việc học. Vậy nên dạo này trẻ con đọc truyện tranh rất nhiều.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Professeur, comment allez-vous ? Mon enfant ne lit pas beaucoup de livres ces jours-ci. J'étais tellement inquiet que je suis venu ici.
Homme : Oui. Veuillez vous asseoir ici. (Il arrête de parler un instant) Uhm... Si votre enfant n'aime pas lire des livres, pourquoi ne lui montrez-vous pas d'abord des bandes dessinées ?
Femme : Des bandes dessinées ? Alors l'enfant n'aimerait-il pas que les bandes dessinées ?
Homme : Ce n'est pas comme ça. Les bandes dessinées aident votre enfant à lire des livres. Si votre enfant trouve intéressant ce qu'il lit dans les bandes dessinées, il trouvera d'autres livres à lire.
Femme : Eh bien, si c'est le cas, c'est bien de pouvoir développer une habitude de lecture.
Homme : Oui. De plus, il est utile d'étudier car il est facile de comprendre des choses difficiles. Les enfants lisent donc beaucoup de bandes dessinées de nos jours.

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Mujer: Maestra, ¿cómo está? Mi hijo no lee mucho libros en estos días. Estaba tan preocupado que vine aquí.
Hombre: Sí. Por favor sientase aquí. (Deja de hablar por un momento) Uhm... Si a tu hijo no le gusta leer libros, ¿por qué no le muestras primero las historietas?
Mujer: ¿Libros de historietas? Entonces, ¿al niño no le gustarían sólo los cómics?
Hombre: No es así. Los cómics ayudan a su hijo a leer libros. Si su hijo encuentra interesante lo que lee en los cómics, encontrará otros libros para leer.
Mujer: Bueno, si es así, es bueno poder desarrollar un hábito de lectura.
Hombre: Sí. Además, es útil estudiar porque es fácil entender cosas difíciles. Así que los niños leen muchos libros de historietas en estos días.

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.id:
Wanita: Guru, bagaimana kabarmu? Anak saya tidak banyak membaca buku akhir-akhir ini. Saya sangat khawatir bahwa saya datang ke sini.
Pria: Ya. Silakan duduk di sini. (Berhenti bicara sebentar) Uhm... Jika anak Anda tidak suka membaca buku, kenapa tidak Anda tunjukkan buku komiknya dulu?
Wanita: Buku komik? Lalu bukankah anak itu hanya menyukai buku komik?
Pria : Bukan seperti itu. Buku komik membantu anak Anda membaca buku. Jika anak Anda menemukan apa yang dia baca di buku komik menarik, dia akan mencari buku lain untuk dibaca.
Wanita: Nah, kalau begitu, ada baiknya kalau bisa membangun kebiasaan membaca.
Pria: Ya. Selain itu, sangat membantu untuk belajar karena mudah untuk memahami hal-hal yang sulit. Jadi anak-anak banyak membaca buku komik hari ini.

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.ja:
女：先生、お元気ですか？最近、うちの子はあまり本を読まなくなりました。悩んでここまで来ました。
男: はい。ここに座ってください。 （ちょっと話が止まる） えっと… 本を読むのが苦手な子なら、先に漫画を見せたら？
女：漫画？だったら子供は漫画しか好きじゃないのでは？
男：そんなことないです。コミックブックは、お子様が本を読むのに役立ちます。子供が漫画で読んだものが面白いと感じたら、他の本を読んでみます。
女：そうですね、それなら読書の習慣が身につくといいですね。
男: はい。また、難しいこともわかりやすく教えてくれるので勉強にも役立ちます。そのため、最近の子供たちはたくさんの漫画本を読んでいます。

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.de:
Frau: Lehrer, wie geht es dir? Mein Kind liest heutzutage nicht mehr viel Bücher. Ich war so besorgt, dass ich hierher kam.

Frau: Comics? Würde das Kind dann nicht nur Comics mögen?
Mann: So ist es nicht. Comic-Bücher helfen Ihrem Kind, Bücher zu lesen. Wenn Ihr Kind das, was es in Comics liest, interessant findet, wird es andere Bücher zum Lesen finden.
Frau: Nun, wenn ja, ist es gut, eine Lesegewohnheit aufbauen zu können.
Mann: Ja. Außerdem ist es hilfreich zu lernen, weil es einfach ist, schwierige Dinge zu verstehen. Kinder lesen heutzutage viele Comics.

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.

g_text_audio_translate.zh-CN:
女：老师，你好吗？我的孩子这几天不怎么看书。我很担心我来到这里。
男：是的。请坐在这里。 （停顿片刻）嗯……如果你的孩子不喜欢看书，你为什么不先给他看漫画书呢？
女：漫画书？那孩子不是只喜欢漫画书吗？
男：不是这样的。漫画书可以帮助您的孩子阅读书籍。如果您的孩子发现他在漫画书中阅读的内容很有趣，他会发现其他书籍可以阅读。
女：嗯，如果是这样，能养成阅读习惯就好了。
男：是的。此外，学习是有帮助的，因为它很容易理解困难的事情。所以现在孩子们读了很多漫画书。

#######

g_text_audio_translate.ru:
Женщина: Учитель, как дела? Мой ребенок сейчас мало читает книги. Я так волновалась, что пришла сюда.

g_text_audio_translate.th:
ผู้หญิง : ครู เป็นยังไงบ้าง? ลูกของฉันไม่ค่อยอ่านหนังสือในช่วงนี้ ฉันกังวลมากจนฉันมาที่นี่
ผู้ชาย: ใช่. กรุณานั่งที่นี่ (หยุดพูดสักพัก) อืม... ถ้าลูกไม่ชอบอ่านหนังสือทำไมไม่เอาหนังสือการ์ตูนให้เขาดูก่อนล่ะ?
ผู้หญิง: หนังสือการ์ตูนเหรอ? แล้วลูกจะชอบแค่หนังสือการ์ตูนไม่ใช่เหรอ?
ผู้ชาย : มันไม่ใช่แบบนั้น หนังสือการ์ตูนช่วยให้ลูกของคุณอ่านหนังสือ หากลูกของคุณพบว่าสิ่งที่เขาอ่านในหนังสือการ์ตูนน่าสนใจ เขาจะหาหนังสือเล่มอื่นให้อ่าน
ผู้หญิง: ถ้าเป็นเช่นนั้น การสร้างนิสัยรักการอ่านก็เป็นเรื่องดี
ผู้ชาย: ใช่. นอกจากนี้การเรียนยังมีประโยชน์เพราะง่ายต่อการเข้าใจเรื่องยากๆ เด็กสมัยนี้จึงอ่านหนังสือการ์ตูนเยอะมาก

g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Mulher: Professora, como vai? Meu filho não lê muito livros atualmente. Fiquei tão preocupado que vim aqui.
Homem: Sim. Por favor, sente-se aqui. (para de falar por um momento) Uhm... Se seu filho não gosta de ler livros, por que você não mostra quadrinhos para ele primeiro?
Mulher: Histórias em quadrinhos? Então a criança não gostaria apenas de histórias em quadrinhos?
Homem: Não é bem assim. Os quadrinhos ajudam seu filho a ler livros. Se seu filho achar interessante o que lê nos quadrinhos, ele encontrará outros livros para ler.
Mulher: Bem, se for assim, é bom poder criar o hábito da leitura.
Homem: Sim. Além disso, é útil estudar porque é fácil entender coisas difíceis. Então, as crianças lêem muitos quadrinhos hoje em dia.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
महिला: टीचर, आप कैसे हैं? मेरा बच्चा आजकल किताबें ज़्यादा नहीं पढ़ता। मैं इतना चिंतित था कि मैं यहां आया।
आदमी: हाँ. कृपया यहीं बैठें. (एक पल के लिए बात करना बंद कर देता है) उह... अगर आपके बच्चे को किताबें पढ़ना पसंद नहीं है, तो आप उसे पहले कॉमिक किताबें क्यों नहीं दिखाते?
महिला: हास्य पुस्तकें? तो फिर क्या बच्चे को सिर्फ कॉमिक किताबें ही पसंद नहीं आएंगी?
आदमी: ऐसा नहीं है. कॉमिक पुस्तकें आपके बच्चे को किताबें पढ़ने में मदद करती हैं। यदि आपका बच्चा कॉमिक पुस्तकों में जो पढ़ता है उसे दिलचस्प लगता है, तो वह पढ़ने के लिए अन्य पुस्तकें ढूंढ लेगा।
महिला: ठीक है, यदि हां, तो पढ़ने की आदत बनाना अच्छी बात है।
आदमी: हाँ. इसके अलावा, यह अध्ययन करने में सहायक है क्योंकि इससे कठिन चीजों को समझना आसान हो जाता है। इसलिए आजकल बच्चे बहुत सारी कॉमिक किताबें पढ़ते हैं।

#######

Woman: Teacher, how are you? My child doesn't read books a lot these days. I was so worried that I came here.
Man: Yes. Please sit here. (Stops talking for a moment) Uhm... If your child doesn't like reading books, why don't you show him comic books first?
Woman: Comic books? Then wouldn't the child only like comic books?
Man: It's not like that. Comic books help your child to read books. If your child find what he reads in comic books interesting, he will find other books to read.
Woman: Well, if so, it's good to be able to build a reading habit.
Man: Yes. In addition, it's helpful to study because it's easy to understand difficult things. So children read a lot of comic books these days.
g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Kadın: Hocam nasılsınız? Çocuğum bu aralar pek kitap okumuyor. Buraya geldiğimde çok endişelendim.
Adam: Evet. Lütfen buraya otur. (Bir an durur) Hımm... Çocuğunuz kitap okumayı sevmiyorsa neden önce ona çizgi roman göstermiyorsunuz?
Kadın: Çizgi romanlar mı? O halde çocuk sadece çizgi romanlardan hoşlanmaz mı?
Adam: Öyle değil. Çizgi romanlar çocuğunuzun kitap okumasına yardımcı olur. Çocuğunuz çizgi romanlarda okuduklarını ilginç buluyorsa okuyacak başka kitaplar da bulacaktır.
Kadın: Eh, eğer öyleyse, okuma alışkanlığını geliştirebilmek iyi bir şey.
Adam: Evet. Ayrıca çalışmak faydalıdır çünkü zor şeyleri anlamak kolaydır. Bu yüzden çocuklar bugünlerde çok fazla çizgi roman okuyor.

explain.vi:
Hãy chọn đáp án tại sao người phụ nữ lại tìm đến người đàn ông.
1. Vì muốn đọc truyện tranh
2. Vì con người phụ nữ học không giỏi
3. V con người phụ nữ không thích đọc sách
4. Vì muốn biết điểm tốt của truyện tranh
--------------------
V + 고 싶어 하다: Cấu trúc thể hiện mong muốn của ngôi thứ 3. 

Nhân vật nữ lo lắng vì con dạo này không hay đọc sách.
explain.en:

<g>V + 고 싶어 하다:</g> Structure expressing the desire of the 3rd person.

general.g_text_audio:
남자: 기타 연습 많이 했어요? 그동안 연습한 거 한번 쳐 볼래요? 
여자: 죄송해요, 선생님. 연습을 많이 못했어요. 
남자: 기타를 잘 치려면 매일 잊지 않고 연습하는 게 중요해요. 
여자: 그건 아는데 연습하는 걸 자꾸 잊어버려요. 
남자: 그럼 기타를 잘 보이는 곳에 두세요. 그러면 기타가 자꾸 보이니까 연습하는 걸 잊지 않겠지요? 
여자: 아, 그럼 저는 기타를 소파 옆에 두어야겠어요. 집에 가면 주로 소파에 앉아서 텔레비전을 보거나 음악을 듣거든요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nam: Em luyện tập guitar nhiều chưa? Em thử đánh một đoạn bài mà dạo này em luyện tập được không? 
Nữ: Em xin lỗi thầy. Em chưa luyện tập nhiều ạ. 
Nam: Nếu muốn đánh guitar giỏi thì điều quan trọng là không quên luyện tập mỗi ngày. 
Nữ: Em cũng biết điều đó nhưng cứ quên mất ạ. 
Nam: Vậy hãy đặt guitar ở nơi dễ nhìn thấy. Nếu vậy thì em sẽ không quên luyện tập vì cứ nhìn thấy guitar hoài đúng không? 
Nữ: À, vậy thì em phải để guitar bên cạnh ghế sofa. Khi về nhà, em chủ yếu ngồi trên ghế sofa để xem ti vi hoặc nghe nhạc.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Man: Did you practice guitar a lot? Do you want to play what you've been practicing?
Woman: I'm sorry sir. I didn't practice much.
Man: If you want to play the guitar well, it's important not to forget to practice every day.
Woman: I know that, but I keep forgetting to practice.
Man: Then put your guitar where you can see it. Then you will keep seeing the guitar, so you won't forget to practice, right?
Woman: Well, then I have to put my guitar next to the sofa. When I go home, I usually sit on the sofa to watch TV or listen to music.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Homme : Avez-vous beaucoup pratiqué la guitare ? Voulez-vous jouer ce que vous avez pratiqué ?
Femme : Je suis désolée monsieur. Je n'ai pas beaucoup pratiqué.
Homme : Si vous voulez bien jouer de la guitare, il est important de ne pas oublier de pratiquer tous les jours.
Femme : Je le sais, mais j'oublie toujours de m'entraîner.
Homme : Alors placez votre guitare là où vous pouvez la voir. Ensuite, vous continuerez à voir la guitare, donc vous n'oublierez pas de pratiquer, n'est-ce pas ?
Femme : Eh bien, je dois mettre ma guitare à côté du canapé. Quand je rentre chez moi, je m'assois généralement sur le canapé pour regarder la télévision ou écouter de la musique.

#######

Man: Did you practice guitar a lot? Do you want to play what you've been practicing?
Woman: I'm sorry sir. I didn't practice much.
Man: If you want to play the guitar well, it's important not to forget to practice every day.
Woman: I know that, but I keep forgetting to practice.
Man: Then put your guitar where you can see it. Then you will keep seeing the guitar, so you won't forget to practice, right?
Woman: Well, then I have to put my guitar next to the sofa. When I go home, I usually sit on the sofa to watch TV or listen to music.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Hombre: ¿Practicaste mucho la guitarra? ¿Quieres tocar lo que has estado practicando?
Mujer: Lo siento señor. No practiqué mucho.
Hombre: Si quieres tocar bien la guitarra, es importante que no te olvides de practicar todos los días.
Mujer: Lo sé, pero sigo olvidándome de practicar.
Hombre: Entonces pon tu guitarra donde puedas verla. Entonces seguirás viendo la guitarra, así que no te olvidarás de practicar, ¿verdad?
Mujer: Bueno, entonces tengo que poner mi guitarra al lado del sofá. Cuando llego a casa, suelo sentarme en el sofá a ver la tele o escuchar música.

g_text_audio_translate.id:
Pria: Apakah Anda banyak berlatih gitar? Apakah Anda ingin memainkan apa yang telah Anda latih?
Wanita : Maaf pak. Saya tidak banyak berlatih.
Pria: Jika Anda ingin bermain gitar dengan baik, penting untuk tidak lupa berlatih setiap hari.
Wanita: Saya tahu itu, tapi saya selalu lupa untuk berlatih.
Pria: Lalu letakkan gitarmu di tempat yang bisa kamu lihat. Kemudian Anda akan terus melihat gitar, sehingga Anda tidak akan lupa untuk berlatih, kan?
Wanita: Nah, kalau begitu saya harus meletakkan gitar saya di sebelah sofa. Ketika saya pulang, saya biasanya duduk di sofa untuk menonton TV atau mendengarkan musik.

男: ギターの練習はたくさんしましたか?練習してきたことを演奏したいですか？
女：すみません。私はあまり練習しませんでした。
男性：ギターを上手に弾きたいなら、毎日練習することを忘れないことが大事です。
女：それはわかってるけど、練習するのを忘れがち。
男：じゃあ、ギターを見えるところに置いて。そうすれば、ずっとギターを見続けるから、練習を忘れないよね？
女性: じゃあ、ギターをソファの横に置かないといけないの。家に帰ると、たいていソファに座ってテレビを見たり、音楽を聴いたりしています。

Frau: Das weiß ich, aber ich vergesse immer wieder zu üben.

Frau: Tja, dann muss ich meine Gitarre neben das Sofa stellen. Wenn ich nach Hause gehe, sitze ich normalerweise auf dem Sofa, um fernzusehen oder Musik zu hören.

Женщина: Ну, тогда мне придется положить гитару рядом с диваном. Когда я иду домой, я обычно сажусь на диван, чтобы посмотреть телевизор или послушать музыку.

g_text_audio_translate.th:
ผู้ชาย: คุณฝึกกีตาร์บ่อยไหม? คุณต้องการที่จะเล่นสิ่งที่คุณได้รับการฝึกฝนหรือไม่?
ผู้หญิง: ฉันขอโทษครับ. ฉันไม่ได้ฝึกซ้อมมากนัก
ผู้ชาย : อยากเล่นกีต้าร์เก่งอย่าลืมฝึกซ้อมทุกวันนะครับ
ผู้หญิง: ฉันรู้ แต่ฉันกลับลืมฝึกซ้อม
ผู้ชาย: งั้นก็เอากีตาร์ไปไว้ในที่ที่คุณมองเห็นสิ แล้วจะคอยดูกีต้าร์เรื่อยๆนะครับไม่ลืมซ้อมใช่ไหมครับ?
ผู้หญิง: คือว่าฉันต้องวางกีตาร์ไว้ข้างโซฟา เมื่อฉันกลับบ้าน ฉันมักจะนั่งบนโซฟาเพื่อดูทีวีหรือฟังเพลง

Homem: Você praticava muito violão? Quer tocar o que tem praticado?
Mulher: Sinto muito, senhor. Eu não pratiquei muito.
Homem: Se você quer tocar violão bem, é importante não esquecer de praticar todos os dias.
Mulher: Eu sei disso, mas sempre esqueço de praticar.

Mulher: Bom, então tenho que colocar meu violão ao lado do sofá. Quando vou para casa, costumo sentar no sofá para assistir TV ou ouvir música.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
आदमी: क्या तुमने गिटार का बहुत अभ्यास किया? क्या आप वही खेलना चाहते हैं जिसका आप अभ्यास कर रहे हैं?
महिला: मुझे माफ कर दीजिए सर. मैंने ज्यादा अभ्यास नहीं किया.
आदमी: यदि आप गिटार अच्छा बजाना चाहते हैं, तो यह महत्वपूर्ण है कि हर दिन अभ्यास करना न भूलें।
महिला: मैं यह जानती हूं, लेकिन मैं अभ्यास करना भूल जाती हूं।
आदमी: तो फिर अपना गिटार वहां रखो जहां से तुम उसे देख सको। फिर आप गिटार देखते रहेंगे तो प्रैक्टिस करना तो नहीं भूलेंगे ना?
महिला: ठीक है, फिर मुझे अपना गिटार सोफे के बगल में रखना होगा। जब मैं घर जाता हूं, तो आमतौर पर टीवी देखने या संगीत सुनने के लिए सोफे पर बैठता हूं।

#######

Woman: I'm sorry sir. I didn't practice much.
Man: If you want to play the guitar well, it's important not to forget to practice every day.
Woman: I know that, but I keep forgetting to practice.

g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Adam: Çok gitar çalıştın mı? Çalıştığınız şeyi oynamak ister misiniz?
Kadın: Özür dilerim efendim. Pek pratik yapmadım.
Adam: Eğer gitarı iyi çalmak istiyorsanız, her gün pratik yapmayı unutmamanız önemlidir.
Kadın: Biliyorum ama pratik yapmayı unutup duruyorum.
Adam: O halde gitarını görebileceğin bir yere koy. O zaman gitarı görmeye devam edeceksin, böylece pratik yapmayı unutmayacaksın, değil mi?
Kadın: Peki o zaman gitarımı kanepenin yanına koymam gerekiyor. Eve gittiğimde genellikle televizyon izlemek veya müzik dinlemek için kanepeye otururum.

#######

Man: Did you practice guitar a lot? Do you want to play what you've been practicing?
Woman: I'm sorry sir. I didn't practice much.
Man: If you want to play the guitar well, it's important not to forget to practice every day.
Woman: I know that, but I keep forgetting to practice.
Man: Then put your guitar where you can see it. Then you will keep seeing the guitar, so you won't forget to practice, right?

content.q_text: 여자가 기타 연습을 자주 못하는 이유를 고르십시오.

explain.vi:
Chọn lý do tại sao bạn nữ không thường xuyên luyện tập guitar.
1. Vì không thích chơi guitar
2. Vì không có thời gian để tập guitar
3. Vì quên mất việc luyện tập guitar
4. Vì không có người dạy guitar 
--------------------
가르치다: Dạy
연습하다: Luyện tập

V + (으)ㄹ 래요?: Cấu trúc rủ rê. 
V/A + 지 않다: Không, chưa. Mang ý nghĩa phủ định. 
V/A + 네요: Đuôi câu cảm thán. 

Vì nhân vật nữ để guitar ở nơi khó nhìn thấy nên nhiều khi quên mất việc luyện tập guitar.
explain.en:
Choose a reason why the woman doesn't practice the guitar often.
1. Because she doesn't like playing guitar
2. Because she does not have time for practicing guitar
3. Because she forgets to practice guitar
4. Because she has no one to teach her to play guitar
--------------------
<g>가르치다:</g> To teach
<g>연습하다:</g> To practice

<g>V + (으)ㄹ 래요?:</g> Do you want to, how about…?
<g>V/A + 지 않다:</g> Used to negate an action or a state.
<g>V/A + 네요:</g> Used to express surprise or admiration.

Because the woman puts the guitar in a place where it is hard to see, she often forgets to practice guitar.
explain.fr:
Choisissez une raison pour laquelle la femme ne pratique pas souvent la guitare.
1. Parce qu'elle n'aime pas jouer de la guitare
2. Parce qu'elle n'a pas le temps de pratiquer la guitare
3. Parce qu'elle oublie de pratiquer la guitare
4. Parce qu'elle n'a personne pour lui apprendre à jouer de la guitare
--------------------
<g>가르치다:</g>Enseigner
<g>연습하다:</g>S'entraîner

<g>V + (으)ㄹ 래요?:</g>Voulez-vous, que diriez-vous de… ?
<g>V/A + 지 않다:</g>Utilisé pour nier une action ou un état.
<g>V/A + 네요:</g>Utilisé pour exprimer la surprise ou l'admiration.

Parce que la femme met la guitare dans un endroit où il est difficile de voir, elle oublie souvent de pratiquer la guitare.

#######

Choose a reason why the woman doesn't practice the guitar often.
1. Because she doesn't like playing guitar
2. Because she does not have time for practicing guitar
3. Because she forgets to practice guitar
4. Because she has no one to teach her to play guitar
--------------------
<g>가르치다:</g> To teach
<g>연습하다:</g> To practice

<g>V + (으)ㄹ 래요?:</g> Do you want to, how about…?
<g>V/A + 지 않다:</g> Used to negate an action or a state.
<g>V/A + 네요:</g> Used to express surprise or admiration.

Because the woman puts the guitar in a place where it is hard to see, she often forgets to practice guitar.
explain.es:
Elija una razón por la cual la mujer no practica la guitarra con frecuencia.
1. Porque no le gusta tocar la guitarra.
2. Porque no tiene tiempo para practicar guitarra
3. Porque se olvida de practicar la guitarra
4. Porque no tiene quien le enseñe a tocar la guitarra
--------------------
<g>가르치다:</g>Enseñar
<g>연습하다:</g>Practicar

<g>V + (으)ㄹ 래요?:</g>¿Quieres, qué tal…?
<g>V/A + 지 않다:</g>Se usa para negar una acción o un estado.
<g>V/A + 네요:</g>Se usa para expresar sorpresa o admiración.

Debido a que la mujer pone la guitarra en un lugar donde es difícil ver, a menudo se olvida de practicar la guitarra.

explain.id:
Pilih alasan mengapa wanita itu tidak sering berlatih gitar.
1. Karena dia tidak suka bermain gitar
2. Karena dia tidak punya waktu untuk berlatih gitar
3. Karena dia lupa latihan gitar
4. Karena tidak ada yang mengajarinya bermain gitar
--------------------
<g>가르치다:</g>Untuk mengajar

general.g_text_audio:
남자: 이번 한국어 말하기 대회에서 1등을 하셨는데요. 기분이 어떠세요? 
여자: 잘하는 학생들이 많았는데 제가 상을 받아서 정말 기뻐요. 
남자: 그렇군요. 실수도 하지 않고 자연스럽게 잘하셔서 정말 놀랐어요. 
여자: 사실 제가 사람들 앞에서 긴장을 많이 해요. 그래서 친구들 앞에서 매일 말하기 연습을 했어요. 또 한국어 선생님께서 발음 연습을 도와주셨어요. 그 덕분에 1등을 한 것 같아요. 
남자: 네, 그런데 이번 대회에는 어떻게 참가하게 되셨어요? 
여자: 이번이 한국에서 공부하는 마지막 학기라서 그동안 해 보지 못한 경험을 하고 싶었어요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nam: Bạn đã giành được giải nhất trong cuộc thi nói tiếng Hàn lần này. Cảm xúc hiện tại của bạn thế nào? 
Nữ: Cuộc thi có rất nhiều bạn học sinh giỏi tham gia, vậy nên tôi thực sự rất vui khi nhận được giải thưởng này. 
Nam: Vậy sao. Tôi thấy ngạc nhiên khi bạn không mắc lỗi nào và nói rất tự nhiên. 
Nữ: Thực ra khi đứng trước nhiều người như vậy tôi cảm thấy rất căng thẳng. Vì vậy mà ngày nào tôi cũng luyện nói trước mặt bạn bè. Bên cạnh đó, giáo viên tiếng Hàn cũng giúp tôi luyện phát âm nữa. Nhờ vậy mà tôi đã giành được giải nhất. 
Nam: Vâng, nhưng lý do bạn tham gia cuộc thi lần này là gì? 
Nữ: Đây là học kỳ cuối cùng của tôi ở Hàn Quốc nên tôi muốn trải nghiệm những điều mà tôi chưa từng làm trong suốt thời gian qua.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Man: You won first prize in this Korean speaking contest. How are you feeling right now?
Woman: There were a lot of good students participating in the contest, so I'm really happy that I received the award.
Man: That's true. I was really surprised that you did well without making any mistakes.
Woman: Actually, I get really nervous in front of people. So, I practiced speaking in front of my friends every day. Also, my Korean teacher helped me practice my pronunciation. Thanks to that, I won first prize.
Man: Yes, but how did you get into this competition?
Woman: This is my last semester studying in Korea, so I wanted to experience something I had never done before.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Homme : Vous avez remporté le premier prix de ce concours de langue coréenne. Comment vous sentez-vous en ce moment ?
Femme : Il y avait beaucoup de bons étudiants qui ont participé au concours, donc je suis vraiment heureuse d'avoir reçu le prix.
Homme : C'est vrai. J'ai été vraiment surpris que vous ayez bien fait sans faire d'erreurs.
Femme : En fait, je deviens vraiment nerveuse devant les gens. Alors, je m'entraînais à parler devant mes amis tous les jours. De plus, mon professeur de coréen m'a aidé à pratiquer ma prononciation. Grâce à cela, j'ai gagné le premier prix.
Homme : Oui, mais comment êtes-vous arrivé à ce concours ?
Femme : C'est mon dernier semestre d'études en Corée, alors je voulais faire l'expérience de quelque chose que je n'avais jamais fait auparavant.

#######

Mujer: En realidad, me pongo muy nerviosa frente a la gente. Entonces, practiqué hablar frente a mis amigos todos los días. Además, mi profesor de coreano me ayudó a practicar mi pronunciación. Gracias a eso, gané el primer premio.
Hombre: Sí, pero ¿cómo llegaste a esta competencia?
Mujer: Este es mi último semestre estudiando en Corea, así que quería experimentar algo que nunca antes había hecho.

女性：コンテストには優秀な学生がたくさん参加していたので、受賞できて本当にうれしいです。
男：そうですね。ミスをせずによくやったことに本当に驚きました。
女：実は人前で緊張するんです。それで、毎日友達の前で話す練習をしました。また、韓国語の先生が発音の練習を手伝ってくれました。おかげさまで一等賞を受賞することができました。

女性: 韓国で勉強する最後の学期なので、今までに経験したことのないことを体験したかったのです。

g_text_audio_translate.th:
ผู้ชาย: คุณได้รับรางวัลชนะเลิศในการแข่งขันพูดภาษาเกาหลีครั้งนี้ ตอนนี้คุณรู้สึกอย่างไร?
หญิง: มีนักเรียนเก่งๆ เข้าร่วมประกวดเยอะมาก ดีใจมากที่ได้รับรางวัล
ผู้ชาย: นั่นเป็นเรื่องจริง ฉันประหลาดใจมากที่คุณทำได้ดีโดยไม่มีข้อผิดพลาดใดๆ
ผู้หญิง: จริงๆ แล้วฉันรู้สึกประหม่ามากเวลาอยู่ต่อหน้าผู้คน ฉันจึงฝึกพูดต่อหน้าเพื่อนทุกวัน ครูสอนภาษาเกาหลีช่วยฉันฝึกออกเสียงด้วย ด้วยเหตุนี้ฉันจึงได้รับรางวัลที่หนึ่ง
ชาย: ใช่ แต่คุณเข้าร่วมการแข่งขันครั้งนี้ได้อย่างไร?
ผู้หญิง: นี่เป็นภาคเรียนสุดท้ายที่ฉันเรียนที่เกาหลี ฉันเลยอยากสัมผัสประสบการณ์ที่ไม่เคยทำมาก่อน

Mulher: Muitos bons alunos participaram do concurso, então estou muito feliz por ter recebido o prêmio.
Homem: Isso é verdade. Fiquei realmente surpreso que você se saiu bem sem cometer erros.
Mulher: Na verdade, fico muito nervosa na frente das pessoas. Então, pratiquei falar na frente dos meus amigos todos os dias. Além disso, meu professor de coreano me ajudou a praticar minha pronúncia. Graças a isso, ganhei o primeiro prêmio.
Homem: Sim, mas como você entrou nessa competição?
Mulher: Este é meu último semestre estudando na Coreia, então queria experimentar algo que nunca tinha feito antes.

#######

Man: You won first prize in this Korean speaking contest. How are you feeling right now?
Woman: There were a lot of good students participating in the contest, so I'm really happy that I received the award.
Man: That's true. I was really surprised that you did well without making any mistakes.
Woman: Actually, I get really nervous in front of people. So, I practiced speaking in front of my friends every day. Also, my Korean teacher helped me practice my pronunciation. Thanks to that, I won first prize.
Man: Yes, but how did you get into this competition?
Woman: This is my last semester studying in Korea, so I wanted to experience something I had never done before.
g_text_audio_translate.hi:
आदमी: आपने इस कोरियाई भाषी प्रतियोगिता में प्रथम पुरस्कार जीता। अभी आप कैसा महसूस कर रहे हैं?
महिला: प्रतियोगिता में बहुत सारे अच्छे छात्रों ने भाग लिया था, इसलिए मैं वास्तव में खुश हूं कि मुझे पुरस्कार मिला।
आदमी: यह सच है. मैं वास्तव में आश्चर्यचकित था कि आपने बिना कोई गलती किए अच्छा प्रदर्शन किया।
महिला: दरअसल, मैं लोगों के सामने बहुत घबरा जाती हूं। इसलिए, मैंने हर दिन अपने दोस्तों के सामने बोलने का अभ्यास किया। इसके अलावा, मेरे कोरियाई शिक्षक ने मुझे अपने उच्चारण का अभ्यास करने में मदद की। इसके लिए धन्यवाद, मैंने प्रथम पुरस्कार जीता।
आदमी: हाँ, लेकिन आप इस प्रतियोगिता में कैसे आये?
महिला: यह कोरिया में पढ़ाई का मेरा आखिरी सेमेस्टर है, इसलिए मैं कुछ ऐसा अनुभव करना चाहती थी जो मैंने पहले कभी नहीं किया था।

Adam: Korece konuşma yarışmasında birincilik ödülünü kazandın. Şimdi nasıl hissediyorsun?
Kadın: Yarışmaya çok sayıda iyi öğrenci katıldı, bu yüzden ödülü aldığım için gerçekten çok mutluyum.
Adam: Bu doğru. Hiç hata yapmadan iyi bir performans sergilemenize gerçekten şaşırdım.
Kadın: Aslında insanların önünde çok gergin oluyorum. Bu yüzden her gün arkadaşlarımın önünde konuşma pratiği yaptım. Ayrıca Korece öğretmenim telaffuzumu geliştirmeme yardımcı oldu. Bunun sayesinde birincilik ödülünü kazandım.
Adam: Evet ama bu yarışmaya nasıl girdin?
Kadın: Bu benim Kore'deki son dönemim, bu yüzden daha önce hiç yapmadığım bir şeyi deneyimlemek istedim.

#######

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 작가님의 책이 외국인들에게 인기가 많습니다. 어떤 책인가요? 
남자: 한국인의 재미있는 생활 모습에 대한 책이에요. 방에 신발을 벗고 들어가거나 음식을 자를 때 가위를 사용하는 것처럼요. 
여자: 그런데 어떻게 이런 책을 쓰셨어요? 
남자: 제가 호텔에서 일했는데, 그때 외국 손님들이 한국에 대한 질문을 많이 했어요. 그래서 이런 책을 생각하게 됐죠. 
여자: 그렇군요. 두 번째 책도 준비하고 계세요? 
남자: 네. 외국인들에게 알려 주고 싶은 것이 아직 많아요. 이번에는 한국의 아름다운 장소를 소개하는 책을 써 보려고요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Sách của anh được rất nhiều người nước ngoài yêu thích. Vậy đây là sách viết về gì vậy ạ? 
Nam: Đây là cuốn sách về nét sinh hoạt thú vị của người Hàn Quốc. Giống như việc cởi giày rồi mới vào phòng hoặc dùng kéo để cắt thức ăn. 
Nữ: Anh đã viết cuốn sách này như thế nào ạ? 
Nam: Tôi từng làm việc tại một khách sạn, lúc đó những vị khách nước ngoài đặt ra rất nhiều câu hỏi về Hàn Quốc. Vì vậy tôi đã nghĩ tới cuốn sách này. 
Nữ: Thì ra là vậy. Anh cũng đang chuẩn bị cho cuốn sách thứ hai đúng không? 
Nam: Vâng. Vẫn còn rất nhiều điều tôi muốn giới thiệu với người nước ngoài. Lần này tôi định sẽ viết một cuốn sách giới thiệu những địa điểm đẹp ở Hàn Quốc.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: Your book is popular with foreigners. What kind of book is it?
Man: This is a book about the interesting life style of Koreans. It's like taking off your shoes before entering a room or using scissors to cut food.
Woman: How did you write this book?
Man: I worked at a hotel, and then foreign guests asked a lot of questions about Korea. So I thought of this book.
Woman: Ah, I see. Are you also preparing for the second book?
Man: Yes. There are still many things I want to tell foreigners. This time, I'm going to write a book that introduces beautiful places in Korea.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Votre livre est populaire auprès des étrangers. Quel genre de livre est-ce?
Homme : C'est un livre sur le style de vie intéressant des Coréens. C'est comme enlever ses chaussures avant d'entrer dans une pièce ou utiliser des ciseaux pour couper de la nourriture.
Femme : Comment avez-vous écrit ce livre ?
Homme : J'ai travaillé dans un hôtel, puis les invités étrangers ont posé beaucoup de questions sur la Corée. Alors j'ai pensé à ce livre.
Femme : Ah, je vois. Êtes-vous également en train de préparer le deuxième livre ?
Homme : Oui. Il y a encore beaucoup de choses que je veux dire aux étrangers. Cette fois, je vais écrire un livre qui présente de beaux endroits en Corée.

#######

Woman: Your book is popular with foreigners. What kind of book is it?

Woman: How did you write this book?
Man: I worked at a hotel, and then foreign guests asked a lot of questions about Korea. So I thought of this book.
Woman: Ah, I see. Are you also preparing for the second book?
Man: Yes. There are still many things I want to tell foreigners. This time, I'm going to write a book that introduces beautiful places in Korea.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Mujer: Tu libro es popular entre los extranjeros. ¿Qué tipo de libro es?
Hombre: Este es un libro sobre el interesante estilo de vida de los coreanos. Es como quitarse los zapatos antes de entrar en una habitación o usar unas tijeras para cortar comida.
Mujer: ¿Cómo escribiste este libro?
Hombre: Trabajé en un hotel y luego los invitados extranjeros hicieron muchas preguntas sobre Corea. Así que pensé en este libro.
Mujer: Ah, ya veo. ¿También te estás preparando para el segundo libro?
Hombre: Sí. Todavía hay muchas cosas que quiero decirles a los extranjeros. Esta vez, voy a escribir un libro que presente hermosos lugares en Corea.

Pria: Ya. Masih banyak hal yang ingin saya sampaikan kepada orang asing. Kali ini, saya akan menulis sebuah buku yang memperkenalkan tempat-tempat indah di Korea.

ผู้หญิง: หนังสือของคุณได้รับความนิยมจากชาวต่างชาติ มันเป็นหนังสือประเภทไหน?
ผู้ชาย: นี่คือหนังสือเกี่ยวกับวิถีชีวิตที่น่าสนใจของชาวเกาหลี เหมือนถอดรองเท้าก่อนเข้าห้องหรือใช้กรรไกรตัดอาหาร
ผู้หญิง: คุณเขียนหนังสือเล่มนี้ได้อย่างไร?
ผู้ชาย: ฉันทำงานที่โรงแรมแห่งหนึ่ง แล้วแขกชาวต่างชาติก็ถามคำถามมากมายเกี่ยวกับเกาหลี ฉันจึงนึกถึงหนังสือเล่มนี้
ผู้หญิง : อ๋อ เข้าใจแล้ว คุณกำลังเตรียมหนังสือเล่มที่สองอยู่หรือเปล่า?
ผู้ชาย: ใช่. ยังมีอีกหลายเรื่องที่อยากบอกชาวต่างชาติ คราวนี้ ฉันจะเขียนหนังสือแนะนำสถานที่สวยงามในเกาหลี

g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Mulher: Seu livro é popular entre os estrangeiros. Que tipo de livro é esse?
Homem: Este é um livro sobre o estilo de vida interessante dos coreanos. É como tirar os sapatos antes de entrar em uma sala ou usar uma tesoura para cortar alimentos.
Mulher: Como você escreveu este livro?
Homem: Eu trabalhava em um hotel e os hóspedes estrangeiros faziam muitas perguntas sobre a Coreia. Então pensei neste livro.
Mulher: Ah, entendo. Você também está se preparando para o segundo livro?
Homem: Sim. Ainda há muitas coisas que quero contar aos estrangeiros. Desta vez, vou escrever um livro que apresenta lugares lindos na Coreia.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
महिला: आपकी किताब विदेशियों के बीच लोकप्रिय है. यह किस तरह की पुस्तक है?
आदमी: यह कोरियाई लोगों की दिलचस्प जीवन शैली के बारे में एक किताब है। यह किसी कमरे में प्रवेश करने से पहले अपने जूते उतारने या भोजन काटने के लिए कैंची का उपयोग करने जैसा है।
महिला: तुमने यह किताब कैसे लिखी?
आदमी: मैंने एक होटल में काम किया और तब विदेशी मेहमानों ने कोरिया के बारे में बहुत सारे सवाल पूछे। तो मैंने इस किताब के बारे में सोचा।
महिला: आह, मैं समझ गयी. क्या आप भी दूसरी किताब की तैयारी कर रहे हैं?
आदमी: हाँ. अभी भी बहुत सी बातें हैं जो मैं विदेशियों को बताना चाहता हूँ। इस बार, मैं एक किताब लिखने जा रहा हूँ जो कोरिया की खूबसूरत जगहों का परिचय देगी।

#######

Woman: Your book is popular with foreigners. What kind of book is it?
Man: This is a book about the interesting life style of Koreans. It's like taking off your shoes before entering a room or using scissors to cut food.
Woman: How did you write this book?
Man: I worked at a hotel, and then foreign guests asked a lot of questions about Korea. So I thought of this book.
Woman: Ah, I see. Are you also preparing for the second book?
Man: Yes. There are still many things I want to tell foreigners. This time, I'm going to write a book that introduces beautiful places in Korea.
g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Kadın: Kitabınız yabancılar arasında popüler. Ne tür bir kitap bu?
Adam: Bu Korelilerin ilginç yaşam tarzını anlatan bir kitap. Bu, bir odaya girmeden önce ayakkabılarınızı çıkarmak veya yiyecekleri kesmek için makas kullanmak gibidir.
Kadın: Bu kitabı nasıl yazdın?
Adam: Bir otelde çalıştım ve yabancı konuklar Kore hakkında birçok soru sordu. Bu yüzden bu kitabı düşündüm.
Kadın: Ah, anlıyorum. İkinci kitaba da hazırlanıyor musunuz?
Adam: Evet. Hala yabancılara anlatmak istediğim çok şey var. Bu sefer Kore'nin güzel yerlerini tanıtan bir kitap yazacağım.

#######

Woman: Your book is popular with foreigners. What kind of book is it?
Man: This is a book about the interesting life style of Koreans. It's like taking off your shoes before entering a room or using scissors to cut food.
Woman: How did you write this book?
Man: I worked at a hotel, and then foreign guests asked a lot of questions about Korea. So I thought of this book.
Woman: Ah, I see. Are you also preparing for the second book?
Man: Yes. There are still many things I want to tell foreigners. This time, I'm going to write a book that introduces beautiful places in Korea.

q_answer: 남자는 호텔에서 일을 한 적이 있습니다.
남자는 외국 사람에게 질문을 많이 했습니다.
남자는 외국인을 위한 책을 여러 권 썼습니다.

explain.vi:
Chọn nội dung đúng với đoạn băng được nghe. 
1. Người đàn ông từng làm việc ở khách sạn. 
2. Người đàn ông hỏi người nước ngoài rất nhiều. 
3. Người đàn ông đã viết một số cuốn sách cho người nước ngoài. 
4. Người đàn ông sẽ viết một cuốn sách về văn hóa nước ngoài trong tương lai. 
--------------------
V(으)ㄴ적이 있다/없다: Đã từng/chưa từng làm gì.
N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서): Vì, cho (ai đó, điều gì).
explain.en:
Choose the answer that matches what you heard.
1. The man have worked at a hotel before.
2. The man asked foreigners a lot.
3. The man has written several books for foreigners.
4. The man will write a book about foreign cultures in the future.
--------------------
<g>V + (으)ㄴ 적이 있다/없다:</g> Have/have not done or tried something before.
<g>N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서):</g> For the sake of, in order to.

<g>V + (으)ㄴ 적이 있다/없다:</g>Avoir/n'avoir pas fait ou essayé quelque chose auparavant.
<g>N + 을/를 위해(서), V + 기 위해(서):</g>Pour, afin de.

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 영화배우 김민수 씨, 요즘은 그림도 그리고 계시는데요. 언제부터 그림을 그리셨어요? 
남자: 3년 전에 영화에서 화가 역할을 한 적이 있어요. 직접 그림을 그려야 해서 배우게 됐는데, 지금까지 취미로 그리고 있어요. 
여자: 그렇군요. 주로 어떤 그림을 그리세요? 
남자: 처음엔 산을 그렸는데 요즘은 사람들의 얼굴을 그리고 있어요. 
여자: 곧 첫 번째 전시회를 하신다고요? 
남자: 네. 같이 그림 그리는 친구들과 전시회를 하기로 했어요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Diễn viên điện ảnh Kim Minsoo, tôi được biết dạo này anh đang vẽ tranh. Anh vẽ tranh từ khi nào vậy? 
Nam: 3 năm trước tôi từng đóng vai họa sĩ trong một bộ phim. Vì phải trực tiếp vẽ tranh nên tôi đã học vẽ, cho đến bây giờ tôi vẫn vẽ tranh như một sở thích của mình. 
Nữ: Thì ra là vậy. Anh thường vẽ tranh gì vậy? 
Nam: Lúc đầu thì tôi vẽ những ngọn núi, nhưng dạo này tôi đang vẽ chân dung người. 
Nữ: Tôi nghe nói anh sắp mở buổi triển lãm đầu tiên đúng không? 
Nam: Đúng vậy. Tôi quyết định mở một cuộc triển lãm với những người bạn cùng vẽ tranh với mình.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: Actor Kim Minsoo, I heard you've been painting these days. When did you start painting?
Man: Three years ago, I played the role of a painter in a movie. I learned to paint because I had to painted by myself, and I've been painting as a hobby until now.
Woman: Oh, I see. What kind of pictures do you usually paint?
Man: At first painted mountains, but now I'm painting portraits.
Woman: You're having your first exhibition soon, right?
Man: That's right. I'm going to have an exhibition with my painting friends.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Acteur Kim Minsoo, j'ai entendu dire que vous peigniez ces jours-ci. Quand avez-vous commencé à peindre ?
Homme : Il y a trois ans, j'ai joué le rôle d'un peintre dans un film. J'ai appris à peindre parce que je devais peindre par moi-même, et j'ai peint comme passe-temps jusqu'à présent.
Femme : Oh, je vois. Quel genre de tableaux peignez-vous habituellement ?
Homme : Au début, je peignais des montagnes, mais maintenant je peins des portraits.
Femme : Vous organisez bientôt votre première exposition, n'est-ce pas ?
Homme : C'est vrai. Je vais faire une exposition avec mes amis peintres.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.es:
Mujer: Actor Kim Minsoo, escuché que has estado pintando estos días. ¿Cuándo empezaste a pintar?
Hombre: Hace tres años, hice el papel de pintor en una película. Aprendí a pintar porque tenía que pintar solo, y he estado pintando como hobby hasta ahora.
Mujer: Oh, ya veo. ¿Qué tipo de cuadros sueles pintar?
Hombre: Al principio pintaba montañas, pero ahora estoy pintando retratos.
Mujer: Vas a tener tu primera exposición pronto, ¿verdad?
Hombre: Así es. Voy a hacer una exposición con mis amigos pintores.

Pria: Itu benar. Saya akan mengadakan pameran dengan teman-teman melukis saya.

ผู้หญิง: นักแสดงคิมมินซู ฉันได้ยินมาว่าช่วงนี้คุณวาดภาพ คุณเริ่มวาดภาพเมื่อไหร่?
ผู้ชาย: เมื่อสามปีที่แล้ว ฉันรับบทเป็นจิตรกรในภาพยนตร์ ฉันเรียนรู้การวาดภาพเพราะฉันต้องวาดภาพด้วยตัวเอง และฉันก็วาดภาพเป็นงานอดิเรกมาจนถึงตอนนี้
ผู้หญิง : อ๋อ เข้าใจแล้ว ปกติคุณวาดภาพแบบไหน?
ผู้ชาย: ตอนแรกวาดภูเขา แต่ตอนนี้ฉันกำลังวาดภาพบุคคล
ผู้หญิง: คุณกำลังจะมีนิทรรศการครั้งแรกเร็วๆ นี้ใช่ไหม?
ผู้ชาย: ถูกต้อง. ฉันจะจัดนิทรรศการกับเพื่อนวาดภาพของฉัน

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
महिला: अभिनेता किम मिनसू, मैंने सुना है कि आप इन दिनों पेंटिंग कर रहे हैं। आपने पेंटिंग कब शुरू की?
आदमी: तीन साल पहले, मैंने एक फिल्म में एक चित्रकार की भूमिका निभाई थी। मैंने पेंटिंग करना सीखा क्योंकि मुझे खुद ही पेंटिंग करनी पड़ती थी और मैं अब तक शौक के तौर पर पेंटिंग करता रहा हूं।
महिला: ओह, मैं समझ गयी. आप आमतौर पर किस प्रकार के चित्र बनाते हैं?
आदमी: पहले मैंने पहाड़ों को चित्रित किया, लेकिन अब मैं चित्र बना रहा हूँ।
महिला: जल्द ही आपकी पहली प्रदर्शनी होने वाली है, है ना?
आदमी: यह सही है. मैं अपने पेंटिंग मित्रों के साथ एक प्रदर्शनी लगाने जा रहा हूं।

#######

Woman: Actor Kim Minsoo, I heard you've been painting these days. When did you start painting?
Man: Three years ago, I played the role of a painter in a movie. I learned to paint because I had to painted by myself, and I've been painting as a hobby until now.
Woman: Oh, I see. What kind of pictures do you usually paint?
Man: At first painted mountains, but now I'm painting portraits.
Woman: You're having your first exhibition soon, right?
Man: That's right. I'm going to have an exhibition with my painting friends.
g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Kadın: Aktör Kim Minsoo, bu aralar resim yaptığını duydum. Ne zaman resim yapmaya başladın?
Adam: Üç yıl önce bir filmde ressam rolünü oynadım. Kendi başıma resim yapmak zorunda kaldığım için resim yapmayı öğrendim ve şu ana kadar hobi olarak resim yapıyordum.
Kadın: Ah, anlıyorum. Genellikle ne tür resimler çizersiniz?
Adam: İlk başta dağları boyadım ama şimdi portreler çiziyorum.
Kadın: Yakında ilk sergini açacaksın değil mi?
Adam: Doğru. Resim arkadaşlarımla bir sergi açacağım.

#######

Woman: Actor Kim Minsoo, I heard you've been painting these days. When did you start painting?
Man: Three years ago, I played the role of a painter in a movie. I learned to paint because I had to painted by myself, and I've been painting as a hobby until now.
Woman: Oh, I see. What kind of pictures do you usually paint?
Man: At first painted mountains, but now I'm painting portraits.
Woman: You're having your first exhibition soon, right?
Man: That's right. I'm going to have an exhibition with my painting friends.

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 한국에는 무슨 일로 오셨어요?
남자: 저는 태권도를 배우러 왔어요. 미국에서도 일 년 정도 배웠는데 아직 잘 못해.요 그래서 더 배우려고요.
여자: 그래요. 저는 한국어를 공부하러 왔어요. 나중에 한국어를 잘하면 한국에 있는 회사에 취직하고 싶어요.
남자: 저는 미국에 돌아가서 태권도를 가르치는 것이 꿈이에요.
여자: 와 멋진데요. 태권도 하는 모습을 한번 보고 싶어요!

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Anh đến Hàn Quốc vì điều gì?
Nam: Tôi tới để học Taekwondo. Tôi đã học ở Mỹ khoảng 1 năm nhưng vẫn chưa giỏi lắm. Vì vậy tôi muốn tới để học hỏi thêm.
Nữ: Vậy sao. Còn tôi đến để học tiếng Hàn. Sau này khi tôi thành thạo tiếng Hàn, tôi muốn xin việc ở công ty Hàn Quốc.
Nam: Ước mơ của tôi là quay về Mỹ và dạy Taekwondo.
Nữ: Oa, thật tuyệt vời. Tôi cũng muốn xem dáng vẻ tập Taekwondo của anh!
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: What made you come to Korea?
Man: I'm here to learn Taekwondo. I studied in the US for about a year, but I'm still not good at it. So I'm trying to learn more.
Woman: Really? I'm here to study Korean. In the future, if I can speak Korean well, I want to get a job at a company in Korea.
Man: My dream is to go back to America and teach Taekwondo.
Woman: Wow, that's great. I want to see you practice Taekwondo!
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Qu'est-ce qui vous a poussé à venir en Corée ?
Homme : Je suis ici pour apprendre le Taekwondo. J'ai étudié aux États-Unis pendant environ un an, mais je ne suis toujours pas bon dans ce domaine. Alors j'essaie d'en savoir plus.
Femme : Vraiment ? Je suis ici pour étudier le coréen. À l'avenir, si je peux bien parler coréen, je veux trouver un emploi dans une entreprise en Corée.
Homme : Mon rêve est de retourner en Amérique et d'enseigner le Taekwondo.
Femme : Wow, c'est super. Je veux te voir pratiquer le Taekwondo !

#######

Woman: What made you come to Korea?
Man: I'm here to learn Taekwondo. I studied in the US for about a year, but I'm still not good at it. So I'm trying to learn more.
Woman: Really? I'm here to study Korean. In the future, if I can speak Korean well, I want to get a job at a company in Korea.
Man: My dream is to go back to America and teach Taekwondo.
Woman: Wow, that's great. I want to see you practice Taekwondo!
g_text_audio_translate.es:

Hombre: Estoy aquí para aprender Taekwondo. Estudié en los EE. UU. durante aproximadamente un año, pero todavía no soy bueno en eso. Así que estoy tratando de aprender más.
Mujer: ¿En serio? Estoy aquí para estudiar coreano. En el futuro, si puedo hablar bien coreano, quiero conseguir un trabajo en una empresa en Corea.
Hombre: Mi sueño es volver a Estados Unidos y enseñar Taekwondo.
Mujer: Wow, eso es genial. ¡Quiero verte practicar Taekwondo!

ผู้ชาย: ฉันมาที่นี่เพื่อเรียนเทควันโด ฉันเรียนที่อเมริกามาประมาณหนึ่งปีแล้ว แต่ก็ยังไม่เก่งอยู่ดี ดังนั้นฉันจึงพยายามเรียนรู้เพิ่มเติม

ผู้ชาย: ความฝันของฉันคือกลับไปอเมริกาเพื่อสอนเทควันโด

आदमी: मैं यहां तायक्वोंडो सीखने आया हूं। मैंने लगभग एक वर्ष तक अमेरिका में अध्ययन किया, लेकिन मैं अभी भी इसमें अच्छा नहीं हूँ। इसलिए मैं और अधिक जानने का प्रयास कर रहा हूं।
महिला: सच में? मैं यहां कोरियाई भाषा का अध्ययन करने आया हूं। भविष्य में, अगर मैं अच्छी तरह से कोरियाई भाषा बोल सकूं, तो मैं कोरिया की एक कंपनी में नौकरी पाना चाहता हूं।
आदमी: मेरा सपना अमेरिका वापस जाकर ताइक्वांडो सिखाने का है।
महिला: वाह, यह तो बढ़िया है. मैं तुम्हें तायक्वोंडो का अभ्यास करते देखना चाहता हूँ!

general.g_text_audio:
남자: 지금 살고 있는 하숙집은 마음에 들어요?
여자: 네, 방도 깨끗하고 하숙집 아주머니도 아주 친절하세요. 그리고 음식을 안 만들어도 돼서 정말 편해요. 맛있는 한국 음식도 매일 먹을 수 있고요.
남자: 불편한 점은 없어요?
여자: 음.......  샤워실이 하나밖에 없어서 좀 불편해요. 그리고 가끔 옆방의 음악 소리가 너무 커서 잠을 못 잘 때가 있어요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nam: Cậu có thích nhà trọ mình đang ở không?
Nữ: Có, phòng sạch sẽ mà cô chủ nhà trọ cũng rất tốt bụng nữa. Với cả không cần nấu ăn nên rất thoải mái. Tớ có thể ăn những món ăn ngon của Hàn Quốc mỗi ngày.
Nam: Không có điều gì bất tiện sao?
Nữ: Ừm... Chỉ có một nhà tắm nên hơi bất tiện. Và thi thoảng phòng bên cạnh bật nhạc to quá làm nhiều lúc tớ không ngủ được.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Man: Do you like the boarding house you live in now?
Woman: Yes, the room is clean and the boarding house's owner is very kind. And it's really convenient because you don't have to cook. You can also eat delicious Korean food every day.
Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Homme : Aimez-vous la pension dans laquelle vous vivez maintenant ?
Femme : Oui, la chambre est propre et la propriétaire de la pension est très gentille. Et c'est vraiment pratique car vous n'avez pas à cuisiner. Vous pouvez également manger de délicieux plats coréens tous les jours.
Homme : Y a-t-il des inconvénients ?
Femme : Ummm...... Il n'y a qu'une seule salle de douche, donc c'est un peu gênant. Et parfois, la musique dans la pièce voisine est si forte que je ne peux pas dormir.

#######

Man: Do you like the boarding house you live in now?
Woman: Yes, the room is clean and the boarding house's owner is very kind. And it's really convenient because you don't have to cook. You can also eat delicious Korean food every day.
Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Hombre: ¿Te gusta la pensión en la que vives ahora?
Mujer: Sí, la habitación está limpia y la dueña de la pensión es muy amable. Y es realmente conveniente porque no tienes que cocinar. También puedes comer deliciosa comida coreana todos los días.
Hombre: ¿Hay algún inconveniente?
Mujer: Ummm...... Sólo hay un baño con ducha, por lo que es un poco incómodo. Y a veces la música en la habitación de al lado es tan alta que no puedo dormir.

#######

Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.id:
Pria: Apakah Anda menyukai rumah kos yang Anda tinggali sekarang?
Wanita: Ya, kamarnya bersih dan pemilik kosnya sangat baik. Dan itu sangat nyaman karena Anda tidak perlu memasak. Anda juga bisa makan makanan Korea yang lezat setiap hari.
Pria: Apakah ada ketidaknyamanan?
Wanita: Ummm...... Hanya ada satu kamar mandi, jadi agak merepotkan. Dan terkadang musik di kamar sebelah sangat keras sehingga saya tidak bisa tidur.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.ja:
男：今住んでいる下宿は好きですか？
女：はい、部屋はきれいですし、下宿のオーナーさんもとても親切です。料理をする必要がないのでとても便利です。毎日美味しい韓国料理も食べられます。
男：何か不便なことはありますか？
女：うーん……シャワールームが一つしかないのでちょっと不便です。また、隣の部屋の音楽がうるさくて眠れないこともあります。

#######

Man: Do you like the boarding house you live in now?
Woman: Yes, the room is clean and the boarding house's owner is very kind. And it's really convenient because you don't have to cook. You can also eat delicious Korean food every day.
Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.de:
Mann: Gefällt dir die Pension, in der du jetzt lebst?
Frau: Ja, das Zimmer ist sauber und der Besitzer der Pension ist sehr nett. Und es ist wirklich praktisch, weil Sie nicht kochen müssen. Sie können auch jeden Tag leckeres koreanisches Essen essen.
Mann: Gibt es irgendwelche Unannehmlichkeiten?
Frau: Ähm...... Es gibt nur einen Duschraum, also ist es etwas umständlich. Und manchmal ist die Musik im Nebenzimmer so laut, dass ich nicht schlafen kann.

#######

Man: Do you like the boarding house you live in now?
Woman: Yes, the room is clean and the boarding house's owner is very kind. And it's really convenient because you don't have to cook. You can also eat delicious Korean food every day.
Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.zh-CN:
男：你喜欢你现在住的宿舍吗？
女：是的，房间很干净，宿舍的主人很和蔼。而且真的很方便，因为你不用做饭。您还可以每天吃美味的韩国料理。
男：有什么不便吗？
女：嗯……淋浴间只有一间，有点不方便。有时隔壁房间的音乐太大声，我无法入睡。

#######

g_text_audio_translate.it:
Uomo: Ti piace la pensione in cui vivi ora?
Donna: Sì, la stanza è pulita e il proprietario della pensione è molto gentile. Ed è davvero conveniente perché non devi cucinare. Puoi anche mangiare deliziosi piatti coreani ogni giorno.
Uomo: Ci sono inconvenienti?
Donna: Ummm...... C'è solo un bagno con doccia, quindi è un po' scomodo. E a volte la musica nella stanza accanto è così forte che non riesco a dormire.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.ru:
Мужчина: Тебе нравится пансионат, в котором ты сейчас живешь?
Женщина: Да, комната чистая, хозяин пансионата очень добрый. И это действительно удобно, потому что не нужно готовить. Вы также можете есть вкусную корейскую еду каждый день.
Мужчина: Есть какие-нибудь неудобства?
Женщина: Ммм... Душевая только одна, так что это немного неудобно. А иногда музыка в соседней комнате такая громкая, что я не могу спать.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.th:
ผู้ชาย: คุณชอบหอพักที่คุณอาศัยอยู่ตอนนี้ไหม?
ผู้หญิง: ใช่ค่ะ ห้องพักสะอาด และเจ้าของหอพักก็ใจดีมาก และสะดวกมากเพราะไม่ต้องทำอาหาร คุณยังสามารถทานอาหารเกาหลีแสนอร่อยได้ทุกวัน
ผู้ชาย: มีความไม่สะดวกอะไรมั้ย?
ผู้หญิง : อืม......ห้องอาบน้ำมีห้องเดียวเลยไม่สะดวกนิดหน่อย และบางครั้งเสียงเพลงในห้องข้างๆก็ดังจนนอนไม่หลับ

#######

Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Homem: Você gosta da pensão onde mora agora?
Mulher: Sim, o quarto é limpo e o dono da pensão é muito gentil. E é muito conveniente porque você não precisa cozinhar. Você também pode comer deliciosa comida coreana todos os dias.
Homem: Há algum inconveniente?
Mulher: Ummm...... Só tem um banheiro, então é um pouco inconveniente. E às vezes a música no quarto ao lado é tão alta que não consigo dormir.

#######

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
आदमी: क्या आपको वह बोर्डिंग हाउस पसंद है जिसमें आप अभी रहते हैं?
महिला: हाँ, कमरा साफ़ है और बोर्डिंग हाउस का मालिक बहुत दयालु है। और यह वास्तव में सुविधाजनक है क्योंकि आपको खाना बनाना नहीं पड़ता है। आप हर दिन स्वादिष्ट कोरियाई खाना भी खा सकते हैं।
आदमी: क्या कोई असुविधा है?
महिला: उम्म्म्म...... वहाँ केवल एक शॉवर कक्ष है, इसलिए यह थोड़ा असुविधाजनक है। और कभी-कभी बगल के कमरे में संगीत इतना तेज़ होता है कि मुझे नींद नहीं आती।

#######

Man: Do you like the boarding house you live in now?
Woman: Yes, the room is clean and the boarding house's owner is very kind. And it's really convenient because you don't have to cook. You can also eat delicious Korean food every day.
Man: Are there any inconveniences?
Woman: Ummm...... There is only one shower room, so it's a bit inconvenient. And sometimes the music in the next room is so loud that I can't sleep.
g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Adam: Şu an oturduğun pansiyonu beğendin mi?
Kadın: Evet, oda temiz ve pansiyonun sahibi de çok nazik. Ve bu gerçekten kullanışlı çünkü yemek pişirmenize gerek yok. Ayrıca her gün lezzetli Kore yemekleri yiyebilirsiniz.
Adam: Herhangi bir sakınca var mı?
Kadın: Hımm...... Sadece bir tane duş odası var o yüzden biraz zahmetli. Ve bazen yan odadaki müzik o kadar yüksek ki uyuyamıyorum.

#######

general.g_text_audio:
남자: 이사할 집은 찾았어요?
여자: 아니요. 아직 못 찾았어요. 저는 교통도 좋고, 근처에 공원이 있어서 산책도 할 수 있는 곳에 살고 싶어요. 그런데 그런 집을 찾기가 좀 힘들어요.
남자: 그럼 저희 아파트에 한 번 가 볼래요? 지하철역 바로 옆인데 ....... 오늘 어때요?
여자: 오늘은 제가 약속이 있어서 안 되는데 ........ 내일 한번 가 볼게요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nam: Chị tìm được nhà để chuyển chưa?
Nữ: Chưa. Tôi vẫn chưa tìm được. Tôi muốn sống ở nơi giao thông thuận tiện và ở gần có công viên để tôi có thể đi dạo. Nhưng tìm nhà như thế thì hơi khó.
Nam: Vậy thì chị muốn thử tới khu chung cư của chúng tôi không? Ga tàu điện ngầm ở ngay bên cạnh... hôm nay chị tới được không?
Nữ: Hôm nay tôi có hẹn nên không đi được rồi.... Mai tôi sẽ thử tới xem sao.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Man: Did you find a house to move in?
Woman: No. I haven't found it yet. I want to live in a place where transportation is good and there is a park nearby where I can take a walk. But it is difficult to find such a house.
Man: Then, would you like to visit my apartment? It's right next to the subway station... How about today?
Woman: I can't because I have an appointment today... But I'll go tomorrow.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Homme : Avez-vous trouvé une maison pour emménager ?
Femme : Non. Je ne l'ai pas encore trouvé. Je veux vivre dans un endroit où les transports sont bons et où il y a un parc à proximité où je peux me promener. Mais il est difficile de trouver une telle maison.
Homme : Alors, voulez-vous visiter mon appartement ? C'est juste à côté de la station de métro... Et aujourd'hui ?
Femme : Je ne peux pas parce que j'ai un rendez-vous aujourd'hui... Mais j'irai demain.

#######

Man: Did you find a house to move in?
Woman: No. I haven't found it yet. I want to live in a place where transportation is good and there is a park nearby where I can take a walk. But it is difficult to find such a house.
Man: Then, would you like to visit my apartment? It's right next to the subway station... How about today?
Woman: I can't because I have an appointment today... But I'll go tomorrow.
g_text_audio_translate.es:

Mujer: No. Aún no lo he encontrado. Quiero vivir en un lugar donde el transporte sea bueno y haya un parque cerca donde pueda caminar. Pero es difícil encontrar una casa así.
Hombre: Entonces, ¿te gustaría visitar mi apartamento? Está justo al lado de la estación de metro... ¿Qué tal hoy?
Mujer: No puedo porque tengo una cita hoy... Pero voy mañana.

#######

Pria: Lalu, apakah Anda ingin mengunjungi apartemen saya? Itu tepat di sebelah stasiun kereta bawah tanah... Bagaimana dengan hari ini?
Wanita: Saya tidak bisa karena saya ada janji hari ini... Tapi saya akan pergi besok.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Mời vào. Anh tìm gì ạ?
Nam: Có bánh kem socola không?
Nữ: Anh thấy cái này thế nào? Đây là chiếc bánh được khách hàng tìm kiếm nhiều nhất thời gian gần đây.
Nam: Vậy sao? Cái đó hơi to nên cho tôi cái bên cạnh đi.
Nữ: À, cái này phải không? Xin lỗi anh. Cái này có khách khác đặt mất rồi ạ.
Nam: Vậy thì cứ cho tôi cái to đi. Bao nhiêu vậy?
Nữ: Của anh 35 000 won.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: Welcome. What are you looking for?
Man: Do you have chocolate cake?
Woman: How about this? This is what most customers are looking for these days.
Man: Is it? It's a little big, so give me the one next to it.
Woman: Oh, this? Sorry. This was ordered by another customer.
Man: Then just give me the big one. How much is it?
Woman: It's 35,000 won.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Bienvenue. Que cherchez-vous?
Homme : Avez-vous un gâteau au chocolat ?
Femme : Et ça ? C'est ce que la plupart des clients recherchent de nos jours.
Homme : C'est ? C'est un peu gros, alors donnez-moi celui d'à côté.
Femme : Oh, ça ? Pardon. Ceci a été commandé par un autre client.
Homme : Alors donne-moi le gros. Combien ça coûte?
Femme : C'est 35 000 wons.

general.g_text_audio:
여자: 가족들이랑 같이 소풍을 가려고 하는데 어디가 좋을까요?
남자: 춘천에 있는 남이섬에 가 보는 게 어때요?
여자: 아, 남이섬요? 예전에 드라마에서 봤어요.
남자: 저는 작년 가을에 가 봤는데 경치가 정말 아름다웠어요.
여자: 그래요? 꼭 가 보고 싶네요. 그런데 어떻게 가야 돼요?
남자: 광화문 앞에서 남이섬 입구까지 버스를 타고 가세요. 2시간쯤 걸릴 거예요.
여자: 가족들하고 거기에서 뭘 하면 좋을까요?
남자: 여러 사람이 함께 탈 수 있는 자전거가 있는데 한번 타 보세요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
Nữ: Tôi đang muốn đi dã ngoại với gia đình, tôi nên đi đâu nhỉ?
Nam: Chị thấy đến đảo Nami ở Chuncheon thế nào?
Nữ: À, đảo Nami hả? Trước đây tôi từng thấy trên phim rồi.
Nam: Năm ngoái tôi cũng thử đến đó rồi, cảnh đẹp lắm.
Nữ: Vậy sao? Tôi cũng muốn đi thử lắm. Nhưng đi tới đó kiểu gì nhỉ?
Nam: Chị đi tới lối vào đảo Nami bằng xe bus trước Gwanghwamun. Sẽ mất khoảng 2 tiếng.
Nữ: Tôi nên làm gì ở đó với gia đình?
Nam: Có xe đạp nhiều người có thể cùng đạp đó, chị thử xem.
g_text_audio_translate.en:
Woman: I want to go on a picnic with my family. Where would be a good place?
Man: Why don't you go to Nami Island in Chuncheon?
Woman: Oh, Nami Island? I saw it in a drama before.
Man: I went there last fall and the scenery was really beautiful.
Woman: Really? I really want to go. How do I get there?
Man: Take a bus from Gwanghwamun to Nami Island entrance. It'll take about two hours.
Woman: What should I do there with my family?
Man: There are bicycles that can be ridden by several people, so try it.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Femme : Je veux aller pique-niquer avec ma famille. Où serait un bon endroit?
Homme : Pourquoi n'irais-tu pas sur l'île de Nami à Chuncheon ?
Femme : Oh, l'île de Nami ? Je l'ai déjà vu dans un drama.
Homme : J'y suis allé l'automne dernier et le paysage était vraiment magnifique.
Femme : Vraiment ? Je veux vraiment y aller. Comment puis-je y arriver?
Homme : Prenez un bus de Gwanghwamun à l'entrée de l'île de Nami. Cela prendra environ deux heures.
Femme : Que dois-je faire là-bas avec ma famille ?
Homme : Il existe des vélos qui peuvent être utilisés par plusieurs personnes, alors essayez-le.

Hombre: Tome un autobús desde Gwanghwamun hasta la entrada de la isla Nami. Tardará unas dos horas.

Hombre: Hay bicicletas que pueden ser montadas por varias personas, así que inténtalo.

Uomo: Ci sono biciclette che possono essere guidate da più persone, quindi provaci.

#######

Woman: I want to go on a picnic with my family. Where would be a good place?
Man: Why don't you go to Nami Island in Chuncheon?
Woman: Oh, Nami Island? I saw it in a drama before.
Man: I went there last fall and the scenery was really beautiful.
Woman: Really? I really want to go. How do I get there?
Man: Take a bus from Gwanghwamun to Nami Island entrance. It'll take about two hours.
Woman: What should I do there with my family?
Man: There are bicycles that can be ridden by several people, so try it.
g_text_audio_translate.ru:

Мужчина: Я был там прошлой осенью, и пейзажи были очень красивыми.

Женщина: Что мне там делать со своей семьей?
Мужчина: Есть велосипеды, на которых могут ездить несколько человек, так что попробуйте.

g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Mulher: Quero fazer um piquenique com minha família. Onde seria um bom lugar?
Homem: Por que você não vai para a Ilha Nami em Chuncheon?
Mulher: Ah, Ilha Nami? Eu já vi isso em um drama antes.
Homem: Fui lá no outono passado e o cenário era realmente lindo.
Mulher: Sério? Eu quero muito ir. Como eu chego lá?
Homem: Pegue um ônibus de Gwanghwamun até a entrada da Ilha Nami. Isso levará cerca de duas horas.
Mulher: O que devo fazer lá com minha família?
Homem: Existem bicicletas que podem ser utilizadas por várias pessoas, então experimente.

g_text_audio_translate.hi:
महिला: मैं अपने परिवार के साथ पिकनिक पर जाना चाहती हूं. इससे अच्छी जगह कहां होगी?
आदमी: आप चुंचियोन में नामी द्वीप क्यों नहीं जाते?
महिला: ओह, नामी द्वीप? मैंने इसे पहले एक नाटक में देखा था।
आदमी: मैं पिछली बार वहाँ गया था और दृश्यावली वास्तव में सुंदर थी।
महिला: सच में? मैं वास्तव में जाना चाहता हूँ। मैं वहां किस प्रकार पहुंचा?
आदमी: ग्वांगवामुन से नामी द्वीप के प्रवेश द्वार तक बस लें। इसमें लगभग दो घंटे लगेंगे.
महिला: मुझे अपने परिवार के साथ वहां क्या करना चाहिए?
आदमी: ऐसी साइकिलें हैं जिन्हें कई लोग चला सकते हैं, इसलिए इसे आज़माएं।
